# Joint Venture Agreement

Party A: Acme AI Inc.

Legal Representative: Jamie Tan, President

Address: 1-6-1 Marunouchi, Chiyoda-ku, Tokyo

Contact: jamie.tan@example.com

Party B: Beacon Trading Corporation

Legal Representative: Chris Lee, Managing Director

Address: 67 Sejong-daero, Jung-gu, Seoul

Contact: chris.lee@example.com

To strengthen cooperation between the parties, and based on the principles of equality, voluntariness, and integrity, Party A and Party B have agreed to jointly invest in the establishment of a joint venture limited liability company (hereinafter referred to as the "Joint Venture") to jointly carry out business activities. Through friendly consultations, the parties have entered into the following agreement:

## I. Establishment of the joint venture

Company Name: Acme-Beacon APAC Holdings Pte. Ltd.

Registered Capital: SGD 10,000,000

Place of Registration: Singapore

Business Scope:General Trading, Project Consulting and Coordination, Commercial Brokerage Services, Market Development and Marketing Planning, Investment Advisory. (Subject to final approval by the company registration authority.)

Term of Operation:15 years. Prior to the expiry of the term of operation, such term may be extended upon the unanimous consent of all shareholders of the Company.

## II. Capital Contribution and Equity Ratio

1. The amount, form and ratio of capital contribution of Party A and Party B are as follows:

1. Party A's subscribed contribution amount is SGD 5,000,000 in the form of currency, all of which shall be contributed to the registered capital, accounting for 50% of the registered capital.

2. Party B's subscribed contribution amount is SGD 5,000,000 in the form of currency, all of which shall be contributed to the registered capital, accounting for 50% of the registered capital.

2. Time of Capital Contribution:

Both parties shall, within 30 business days after the opening of the Joint Venture Company's bank account, remit the initial capital contribution (not less than 20% of their respective subscribed capital) into the account of the Joint Venture Company; the remaining part shall be fully paid up before the issuance of the business license.

Capital Contribution Certificate:
After receiving the full amount of capital contributions, the Joint Venture Company shall engage a local auditor to issue a capital verification report and shall issue Capital Contribution Certificates to both parties.

## III. Business Purpose and Strategic Positioning

The Parties confirm that the core purpose of establishing the Joint Venture Company is to build a regional strategic platform:

Market Development: To conduct market research, brand promotion, and channel development in the Southeast Asia by leveraging the resources of both Parties.

Project Bridging: To provide business opportunity referrals and assist clients in communication and negotiation with local governments or enterprises.

Preliminary Coordination: To serve as a window for the Parties' cooperation in the Southeast Asia, coordinating various matters in the preliminary stages of projects.

For the purposes of this Agreement, the relevant commercial contracts negotiated as from January 1, 2026 shall be signed with Party A prior to the establishment of the joint venture company, provided that the actual benefits of the projects shall belong to the joint venture company being established, and the distribution method shall be as set forth in the joint venture agreement.

## IV. Profit Distribution

1. Distribution Principle: The joint venture shall distribute its distributable profits after taxation for the current year according to the proportion of capital contributions subscribed by both parties.

2. Timing of Distribution: Profit distribution shall be carried out within 60 days after the submission of the annual audit report, the convening of the shareholders' meeting, and the adoption of the dividend resolution.

3. Reserve Fund: Prior to any profit distribution, the Joint Venture Company shall set aside such reserves as required under the Singapore Companies Act and the Company’s Articles of Association.

## V. Corporate Governance Structure

Shareholders' Meeting:

The Shareholders' Meeting, composed of all shareholders, shall be the supreme governing body of the Company. The Shareholders' Meeting hereby authorizes the Board of Directors to assume full responsibility for the day-to-day management, operations, and business decision-making of the Company. The Shareholders' Meeting shall retain decision-making authority over the following material matters:

1. Amendments to the articles of association;

2. Increases or decreases in registered capital;

3. Mergers, divisions, dissolution, or change of corporate form; and;

4. Profit distribution plans and loss recovery plans. resolutions on the foregoing matters shall require the unanimous consent of all shareholders;

Other functions and powers, discussion methods, and voting procedures of the Shareholders' Meeting shall be governed by the Singapore Companies Act and the Company's Articles of Association.

Board of Directors:

The Company shall establish a Board of Directors, which shall consist of 7 directors. 3 director(s) shall be appointed by Party A, and 3 director(s) shall be appointed by Party B. The Chairman of the Board (1 seat) shall be nominated by Acme AI Inc. and elected by the Board of Directors.

The shareholders hereby authorize the Board of Directors to be responsible for the Company's business operations and to exercise decision-making authority. The specific powers of the Board shall include, but not be limited to:

1. Formulating the company's business plans and investment strategies;

2. Reviewing and approving the company's annual financial budgets and final accounts;

3. Reviewing profit distribution plans and loss recovery plans, and submitting them to the shareholders' meeting for approval;

4. Reviewing proposals to increase or decrease registered capital, and submitting them to the shareholders' meeting for approval; and;

5. Appointing or dismissing the general manager and the chief financial officer, and determining their remuneration. board resolutions shall be made jointly by all directors and shall be valid upon approval by a majority of all directors. matters relating to profit distribution and changes in registered capital shall require the unanimous consent of all directors;

Management: The General Manager (CEO) shall be responsible for the daily operations of the Company, and the Chief Financial Officer (CFO) shall be responsible for financial monitoring and control.

## VI. Confidentiality Clause

1. Both Parties shall keep confidential the contents of this Agreement and any commercial, financial, technical, project-related information, user data, or other documents or information received from the other Party during the performance of this Agreement that are marked confidential (collectively, the "Confidential Information"). Neither Party shall disclose such Confidential Information to any third party outside this Agreement without the prior written consent of the Party disclosing the information (the "Disclosing Party"), except where such disclosure is made:

1. To employees of the receiving Party who have a need to know such Confidential Information solely for the purposes of this Agreement, provided that the receiving Party instructs such employees to comply with the confidentiality and non-disclosure obligations set forth in this Clause.

2. If the documents and information are publicly available, or if their disclosure is required by applicable laws or regulations.
3. To a court or pursuant to any pre-trial discovery procedure or similar procedure, or in connection with any legal proceedings taken in relation to this Agreement.
4. To a regulatory authority, as required by such regulatory authority.
5. In any other circumstances where disclosure is mandatory by law.

2. Neither Party shall disclose to any third party the contents of this Agreement or any business information of the other Party obtained during the performance of this Agreement, unless with the written consent of the other Party.

3. This confidentiality obligation shall survive the expiration, rescission, or termination of this Agreement.

## VII. Representations and Warranties

Each Party severally and independently represents, warrants, and undertakes to the other Party, with respect to itself only:

1. It is a legal entity duly incorporated and validly existing under the laws of its jurisdiction of incorporation, and it and its authorized representatives have obtained all necessary authorizations and approvals to execute, deliver, and perform this Agreement.

2. It has the power, authority, and right to execute and deliver this Agreement and to comply with its obligations hereunder.

3. Upon effectiveness, this Agreement constitutes valid and legally binding obligations on it, enforceable against it in accordance with its terms and the laws of Republic of Singapore.

4. The execution and delivery of this Agreement does not in any respect violate its articles of association or similar constitutional documents, nor any agreement or contract to which it is a party.

## VIII. Compliance

**General Compliance.** Each Party undertakes that, in the performance of this Agreement, it shall strictly comply with all applicable laws and regulations, including those of its respective place of incorporation and places of business operations, as well as the U.S. Export Administration Regulations (EAR), the International Traffic in Arms Regulations (ITAR), the laws of Singapore, the laws of Japan, and other applicable import and export control laws.

**Independent Liability.** Each Party shall independently bear responsibility for its own legal compliance. In the event that either Party is found to be in violation of any applicable laws or regulations, such Party shall solely bear all legal liabilities and associated risks arising therefrom. The other Party shall not be held jointly or severally liable for, or be deemed to have any direct connection with, such non-compliance.

**Right to Suspend.** If either Party has reasonable grounds to believe that the other Party's conduct may involve a risk of non-compliance with applicable laws or regulations, it shall have the right to notify the other Party in writing and, if necessary, suspend the performance of relevant cooperative activities under this Agreement until such compliance concerns have been satisfactorily resolved.

## IX. Liability for Breach of Contract

1. If either Party fails to make its capital contribution in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement, such defaulting Party shall pay to the non-breaching Party liquidated damages in the amount of SGD 1,000,000 per mille of the overdue contribution for each day of delay. If the delay exceeds 45 days, the non-breaching Party shall have the right to terminate this Agreement and demand that the defaulting Party compensate for the losses incurred.

2. If either Party breaches its confidentiality obligations, it shall pay to the other Party liquidated damages in the amount of SGD 1,000,000. If such amount is insufficient to cover the losses suffered, the breaching Party shall also compensate for all such losses.

## X. Force Majeure

1. Force majeure referred to in this agreement refers to events such as earthquakes, floods, wars, government actions, and other unforeseeable events whose consequences cannot be reasonably prevented or avoided.

2. If this agreement cannot be performed due to force majeure, the affected party shall immediately notify the other party and provide relevant evidence. Based on the impact of the force majeure, both parties shall negotiate to postpone the performance or terminate the agreement.

## XI. Applicable Law and Dispute Resolution

## Governing Law

**Applicable Law:** This Agreement is subject to the laws of Republic of Singapore.

## Dispute Resolution

**Dispute Resolution:** Any dispute arising from this Agreement shall be resolved through friendly negotiation between the parties; if the negotiation fails, it shall be submitted to SIAC for arbitration. The ruling shall be final and binding on both parties.

## Anti-Corruption and Compliance

Each Party represents and warrants that it shall comply with all applicable anti-bribery and anti-corruption laws, including the U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act (FCPA) and the UK Bribery Act 2010, and the laws of Singapore, the laws of Japan.

Neither Party shall, directly or indirectly, make any payment, gift, or offer of value to any government official, political party, or public entity in connection with the Project.

Each Party shall bear sole responsibility for ensuring its own conduct, and that of its employees and agents, complies with all applicable laws.


## XII. Notices

**Notices and Delivery.** Any notice required under this Agreement shall be in writing and sent by personal delivery, registered mail, or email to the addresses specified herein. Personal delivery is deemed delivered on the date of delivery; registered mail on the date of mailing; email when successfully sent.

**Change of Notice Information.** If any Party's notice information changes, it shall notify the other Party in writing within 5 business days.

## XIII. Miscellaneous

This Agreement shall become effective as of the date of its execution.

This Agreement is executed in two counterparts, with each Party holding one copy. Both copies shall have the same legal effect.

Matters not covered herein may be separately negotiated and determined by the Parties, and a supplementary agreement shall be concluded. Any supplementary agreement duly executed by the Parties shall have the same legal effect as this Agreement.

**Conflict.** If there is any inconsistency between the Joint Venture Company's articles of association and this Agreement, this Agreement shall prevail as between the Parties.

**Amendment:** This Agreement may only be amended by a written instrument signed by both Parties.

**Severability:** If any provision of this Agreement is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions shall continue in full force and effect.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the Parties have executed this Agreement as of 2026-05-01.
